text: You place the ring into your pack and the nest on the ground.

text: You see nothing in the nest.

text: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall. In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
speaker: narrator

text: That's what happens sometimes when you go out on a limb.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!"
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!"
speaker: brauggi

text: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
speaker: narrator

text: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
speaker: narrator

text: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't offer what you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
Bringing this brave one to barter so bold.
I journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
Source of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet."
speaker: brauggi
brauggi

text:
"Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor,
Apples or oranges, peaches or pears."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,
That brings joy to Giants and madness to men."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field,
A jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Bored I become when the banter so blindly
Speaks of the subjects I care not about."
speaker: brauggi

text: "Annoy me not over matters so minor."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;
My axe it is asking for action and more."
speaker: brauggi

text: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.
speaker: narrator

text: The snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.
speaker: narrator

text: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see a gem here.
speaker: narrator

text: Brauggi has departed on the long journey to his homeland.
speaker: narrator

text: Brauggi has become impatient with your questions and gone elsewhere.  You will have to return another time.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me."
speaker: brauggi

text:
Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
My axe it is edged as is the ice.
This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood."
speaker: brauggi

text: Next time, pick on someone your own size.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Courage you carry, but skill it is scanty;
Face not this foe before fiercer you fight."
speaker: brauggi

text: Your fruit definitely makes a generous pile in the Giant's cupped hands, but they're not full yet.
speaker: narrator

text: "Oops!"  you say.  "I guess I don't have any fruit for you.  Sorry!"
say: Oops! I guess I don't have any fruit for you. Sorry!
speaker: hero

text: Your meagre selection of fruit makes a paltry pile at the bottom of the Giant's cupped hands.  You'll need to get quite a few more apples to fill THOSE hands!
speaker: narrator

text: As you start to throw...
speaker: narrator

text: As you begin to cast your spell...
speaker: narrator

text: "In matters of magic, I am your master; Seek not to slay me with sendings so small."
speaker: brauggi

text: Running on the ice is apparently not a very good idea.
speaker: narrator

text: The straw is relatively fresh and looks more comfortable to lie upon than the rocks.

speaker: narrator

text: You see the shaggiest little man (you think it's a man, anyway) you've ever seen.
speaker: narrator

text: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!
speaker: narrator

text: The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.
speaker: narrator

text: The phosphorescent fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.
speaker: narrator

text: It's on the table.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't share what you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: "Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations.  When you want to sleep, just say so."
speaker: hermit

text: "Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to come back and sleep tonight since you've shared your rations."
speaker: hermit

text: You can't sleep in this old rickety chair.  Get up and sleep on the bed of straw.
speaker: narrator

text: Get closer to the bed of straw.
speaker: narrator

text: You need to get closer to the chair.
speaker: narrator

text: You are already sitting... on your brain, perhaps?
speaker: narrator

text: You're not sitting.
speaker: narrator

text: The 'ermit wouldn't like that.
speaker: narrator

text: You should wait until the hermit is asleep to check his place out.
speaker: narrator

text: Despite your intentions, you can't bring yourself to spend any more time playing with the talkative 'Enry.
speaker: narrator

text: You spend an hour at the game while 'Enry talks.  You've obviously made his day.
speaker: narrator

text: You should sit down first.
speaker: narrator

text: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.   You now have the knowledge to cast a "Trigger" spell.
speaker: narrator

text: You see no scroll.
speaker: narrator

text: You can detect a faint aura of magic here.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no need to use magic to open anything here.
speaker: narrator

text: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.
speaker: narrator

text: You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
speaker: narrator

text: "Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there. You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage."
speaker: hermit

text: "If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you.  It's just getting molding in me 'iding place."
speaker: hermit

text: "I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you."
speaker: hermit

text: "Do you want it?"
speaker: hermit

text: "Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime."
speaker: hermit

text: "You already 'ave me only scroll."
speaker: hermit

text: "Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?"
speaker: hermit

text: "Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes."
speaker: hermit

text: " 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!"
speaker: hermit

text: "There you 'as it."
speaker: hermit

text: "I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale."
speaker: hermit

text: "Well, see'en as I don't get out much, I don't know much."
speaker: hermit

text: "If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage."
speaker: hermit

text: After having traded a ration, which the 'ermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.
speaker: narrator

text: Unfortunately, since the fungus on the walls shines at night so the room doesn't get dark, and the fact the hermit talks most of the night, you only get five hours rest.
speaker: narrator

text: "You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me."
speaker: hermit

text: So you says to yourself, "Self", you says...
speaker: narrator

text: You face the main doors of the Baron's keep.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle grounds look like no one has taken care of them.  The castle is still impressive close up, but it looks rather dirty

text: The doors are securely locked for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: The doors are guarded by Frederick and Pierre.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle walls are very high.  You judge that you would not be able to climb them.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no response.
speaker: narrator

text: The doors are securely locked and barred from the inside.
speaker: narrator

text: Plays a mean horn.  A member in good standing of the musicians guild.

text: You don't think the guards would allow you to do so.
speaker: narrator

text: No sooner do you draw your weapon then the guards have their weapons out and quickly disarm you.  It seems that they practice with the Baron's Weapons Master.  They promptly drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, which is not a very likely spot for the attainment of hero status.
speaker: narrator

text: The horns are tarnished and starting to corrode.
speaker: narrator

text: The guards look to be middle-aged, but in good physical condition.  Their clothing is faded and patched.  Even the horns look dull.
speaker: narrator

text: The guards are ignoring you.
speaker: narrator

text: He's very proud of his moustache, but don't mention his bald spot.
speaker: narrator

text: Doorway to castle great hall.
speaker: narrator

text: The torch lights the way to the Baron's Hall.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't reach the windows from these trees.
speaker: narrator

speaker: narrator

text: This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
speaker: narrator

text: These flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
speaker: narrator

text: Granite from our very own mountains?
speaker: narrator

text: The Baron von Spielburg and Baronet await you in the great hall.

text: "No one enters the castle without the Baron's permission."

text: "The barracks are off to my right, but you have no business going there."

text: I told you not to mention it.  Now you've hurt his feelings, and he won't talk until he gets over it.

text: "I can't help you on that."

text: After a wonderful meal with the Baron and his son, a peaceful night's sleep in a featherdown bed, and a filling breakfast in bed, you are ready to go adventuring once more.
speaker: narrator

text: This is not what you expected the brigands' fortress to look like in your imagination.  The barracks with the open half-doors to the east and west are fairly normal looking.  The carpet lying near the gate, however, is a bit odd.  The small chasm in the middle of this yard with the bridge across it seems strange, too.  There are two blockades near the gate and some barriers near the north.
speaker: narrator

text: That's interesting.  There seems to be a pit trap under the carpet.
speaker: narrator

text: The carpets look rather new, perhaps recently stolen from the merchant in town.  There is a sign on the blue carpet which reads, "Step Here".
speaker: narrator

text: The barracks have open half-doors and you can hear noise behind them.
speaker: narrator

text: The chasm is about six feet across and ten feet deep.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a dark rope stretched tautly across the path.  It can easily be avoided by stepping over it.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like a plain, simple, harmless box to you.
speaker: narrator

text: The bridge looks sturdy resting on its pole across the chasm.

text: The bridge looks sturdy resting on its poles across the chasm.  There is a sign on it which reads, "Cross Here".
